title: Missile production push runs into solid rocket motor bottleneck
summary: A CSIS report highlights a bottleneck in solid rocket motor production, threatening planned 2027 interceptor purchases due to a recovering supply chain after years of consolidation.

title: Revised Artemis lunar lander plans take shape
summary: NASA has outlined updated strategies for Blue Origin and SpaceX to expedite the development of Artemis lunar landers. The revisions aim to accelerate progress for upcoming lunar missions.

title: SpaceX shares rise nearly 20% in historic IPO
summary: SpaceX shares surged nearly 20% on their first day of trading following the company's historic IPO, marking a significant milestone for both SpaceX and the space industry.

title: Avanti trims GEO exposure with Hylas-3 sale
summary: Avanti Communications is selling its Hylas-3 satellite to reduce its geostationary orbit (GEO) exposure. This move marks a shift away from its previous debt-fueled expansion strategy.

title: Astronomers fear orbital data centers will interfere with observations
summary: Astronomers are concerned that SpaceX's planned orbital data center satellites, set to launch as early as next year, could interfere with astronomical observations. The initiative raises questions about the balance between commercial space activities and scientific research.

title: All in on AI at Astra
summary: Astra is focusing heavily on AI, with CEO Chris Kemp suggesting he might be the last employee remaining at the company. The move comes amid challenges following the SPAC boom.

title: H3 successfully returns to flight
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight on June 11, launching six small satellites into orbit. This marks its first mission since a failure in December.

title: In aerospace, AI isn’t replacing workers. It’s filling a shortage
summary: Aerospace executives view AI as a solution to workforce shortages rather than a replacement for workers. The technology is seen as essential for addressing an overstretched industrial base in the space industry.

title: AAC Clyde Space wins ESA contract to complete maritime-monitoring constellation
summary: AAC Clyde Space secured a €10.9 million contract from ESA to develop and demonstrate VDES satellites for maritime monitoring. The contract aims to complete a maritime-monitoring constellation.

title: Vandenberg offers new launch site for small and medium rockets
summary: The U.S. Space Force is offering a new launch site at Vandenberg Space Force Base for small and medium rockets. The initiative aims to attract potential users to the newly designated facility.

title: Catch Mercury shining at its best on June 15 before it slips back into the sun's glare
summary: Mercury will reach its farthest point from the sun in its current evening apparition on June 15, offering a rare opportunity to observe the planet before it becomes obscured by the sun's glare.

title: How Jules Verne predicted the Artemis 2 mission to the moon almost 160 years ago
summary: Jules Verne's 1860s novels 'From the Earth to the Moon' and 'All Around the Moon' predicted key elements of NASA's Artemis 2 mission, including a conical capsule and a free-return trajectory around the moon. The works, once considered speculative fiction, now appear eerily prescient.

title: New moon of June 2026 brings stunning views of Mercury, Venus and Jupiter
summary: The new moon on June 14, 2026, will offer optimal viewing conditions for planets like Mercury, Venus, and Jupiter, along with constellations in the late spring sky. This astronomical event provides a rare opportunity for stargazers to observe celestial bodies with minimal moonlight interference.

title: 'We were astonished': Millions of exoplanets could be born near active supermassive black holes
summary: Millions of exoplanets could form near active supermassive black holes, according to a new study. Researchers expressed surprise at the potential scale of planet formation in these extreme environments.

title: Millions could see a rare sunset during the total solar eclipse on Aug. 12, 2026. Here's where to look
summary: A total solar eclipse on August 12, 2026, will be visible across parts of the Northern Hemisphere, offering a rare celestial event for millions. Observers are advised to plan ahead to witness the spectacle.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 214 — Moon Man
summary: The latest episode of 'This Week In Space' features a discussion with former NASA Administrator Jim Bridenstine about his past and the future of the space agency. The podcast episode is Episode 214.

title: Closer every day: A spectacular 30-day collage of the Venus-Jupiter conjunction (photo)
summary: A 30-day collage showcases the apparent convergence of Venus and Jupiter in Earth's sky, despite their actual increasing distance in space.

title: How Japanese scientists sent a real-life Transformer to the moon
summary: Japanese scientists deployed an autonomous transforming rover on the moon in 2024, which changed from a sphere into a robot to explore lunar terrain.

title: Ever have a scary HR meeting on your calendar? That's how the Artemis 3 crew found out their assignments
summary: NASA informed the Artemis 3 crew of their assignments in an unconventional way, akin to a scary HR meeting. The astronauts were notified of their roles in an unexpected manner.

title: SpaceX's stock wasn't the company's only launch today — It also put 29 Starlink satellites in orbit
summary: SpaceX launched 29 Starlink satellites into orbit using a Falcon 9 rocket, coinciding with its public debut on the NASDAQ stock market.

title: NASA to Cover 34th SpaceX Resupply Mission Space Station Departure
summary: NASA will cover the departure of SpaceX's Dragon spacecraft from the International Space Station on June 16, returning scientific research samples and hardware to Earth. Live coverage will be available on NASA+, Amazon Prime, and NASA's YouTube channel.

title: Black Eye Galaxy
summary: NASA's Hubble and James Webb Space Telescopes captured a composite image of the Black Eye Galaxy (Messier 64) in multiple wavelengths. The galaxy was observed in near-, mid-, and far-infrared, as well as ultraviolet and visible light.

title: Hubble Sees Swarm of Galaxies
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the galaxy cluster MACS0329-0211, resembling a swarm of bees returning to a hive. This observation contributes to our understanding of galaxy formation and evolution.

title: World Cup Fever in Guadalajara
summary: Guadalajara's metro area has expanded westward since hosting World Cup matches in 1986, with the landscape influenced by ancient volcanoes.

title: NASA Award Boosts Space Technology Research Capabilities
summary: NASA opened a new funding opportunity, M-STAR, to support academic research and technology development in space technology. The application window closes on August 11.

title: NASA’s Chandra Discovers Possible Supernova Remnant in Galactic Center
summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory has discovered a possible supernova remnant in the Galactic Center using new data. The findings, published in The Astrophysical Journal, highlight the expanding remains of an exploded star rich in elements like iron and oxygen.

title: I Am Artemis: Elkin Norena
summary: Elkin Norena, NASA's resident management officer for the Space Launch System Program, has overseen more than a dozen space shuttle missions. His work has contributed to historic missions and crewed flights to low Earth orbit.

title: NASA Robotic Tech Demo Will Advance Prototype Gamma-Ray Detectors
summary: NASA is advancing prototype gamma-ray detectors through a robotic arm demonstration on its upcoming Fly Foundational Robots mission. The new gamma-ray sensor technology aims to enhance scientific capabilities in space exploration.

title: Soccer Meets Space Science
summary: A soccer ball was tested on the International Space Station to study how internal mass affects motion and stability in microgravity. The findings aim to improve understanding of embedded technologies in soccer balls, including match-ball sensors.

title: NASA’s Career Technical Education Day Highlights Technical Careers
summary: NASA hosted Career Technical Education Day to highlight technical careers and cultivate the next generation of skilled workers for its missions and research. The event emphasized the critical role of technicians in engineering, fabrication, electronics, and other technical fields.

title: Booster 20 Completes Cryo, As Ship 40 Nears Static Fire
summary: Booster 20 has completed cryogenic testing, while Ship 40 is approaching static fire tests. Flight 12 was completed about three weeks ago.

title: China successfully debuts tallest rocket, LandSpace prepares for second landing attempt
summary: China successfully launched its tallest rocket, marking a significant milestone in its space program. LandSpace is preparing for a second attempt at a rocket landing.

title: Japan’s H3 to returns to flight with debut launch of lightest configuration
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight with its debut launch of the lightest configuration. The mission marks a significant milestone for Japan's space program after previous setbacks.

title: Stoke Space completes Nova Stage 1 structural verification
summary: Stoke Space has completed the proto-qualification of the first stage of its Nova rocket, verifying its structural integrity. This marks a significant milestone in the development of the company's reusable launch vehicle.

title: Artemis III Crew Named: Veteran Astronauts to test Lunar Landers in LEO
summary: NASA has named the four astronauts who will fly the Artemis III mission, which will test lunar landers in low Earth orbit. This marks a significant step toward the planned crewed lunar landing.

title: Booster 20 into Cryogenic Testing ahead of Starship Flight 13
summary: SpaceX's Starbase team has begun cryogenic testing on Booster 20 in preparation for Starship Flight 13. This follows the recent conclusion of Starship Flight 12.

title: Launch Preview: Chinese, Rocket Lab, and Starlink flights scheduled
summary: The upcoming week includes space launches from China, Rocket Lab, and Starlink, with three Starlink missions and flights from California, Virginia, and Florida. The schedule highlights multiple international and commercial spaceflight activities.

title: NASA concludes MAVEN mission at Mars, uses data to discover new atmospheric phenomenon
summary: NASA has concluded the MAVEN mission at Mars after a six-month struggle to restore communication. The mission's data has led to the discovery of a new atmospheric phenomenon.

title: After New Glenn anomaly, Blue Origin keeps focus on upcoming Blue Moon and Mars missions
summary: Blue Origin remains committed to its upcoming Blue Moon and Mars missions despite the loss of its New Glenn rocket during a hotfire test at Launch Complex 36. The company is focusing on recovery and future objectives.

title: The Exploration Company advances Nyx parachute recovery
summary: The Exploration Company (TEC) has made progress in developing the parachute recovery system for its reusable Nyx spacecraft. This advancement is a key milestone for the spacecraft's reusability and recovery capabilities.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are contributing to ESA's missions by testing and selecting materials capable of withstanding the harsh conditions of space. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section relies on their innovative ideas to ensure mission success.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance efficiency and accessibility in space-related activities.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical parachute testing process for a space mission, likely involving extreme thermal conditions. This bake-out procedure ensures the parachute's integrity before deployment.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter diameter parachute for Mars landing by sterilizing it to prevent microbial contamination. The parachute, the largest ever for Mars, will slow the rover's descent during its 2028 launch and search for signs of life.

title: VCs Predict the SpaceX IPO Will Lift the Entire Industry
summary: Venture capitalists anticipate that SpaceX's potential IPO will positively impact the broader space industry by boosting investor confidence and attracting more capital. The event could shift the industry's reliance on private funding toward public markets.

title: Op-ed: Up to a Trillion Dollars is About to Chase Space. What Kind of Economy Will Be Built?
summary: A trillion dollars is expected to flow into the space industry, raising questions about the nature of the economy that will emerge from this investment. The largest IPO in history is poised to drive record capital into space ventures.

title: What the SpaceX IPO Means for Europe’s Space Industry
summary: SpaceX's IPO is a significant event for the global space industry, with major implications for Europe's space sector. The move could reshape competition and investment dynamics in the industry.

title: Exclusive: Tilebox Launches AI-Focused Update
summary: Tilebox has launched an AI-focused update to enhance the effectiveness of AI agents as geospatial-data analysts. This update aims to improve data processing and analysis capabilities in the space industry.

title: Analysts Debate SpaceX’s $1.75T Valuation Ahead of IPO
summary: Analysts are divided on SpaceX's $1.75 trillion valuation ahead of its potential IPO, with debates over its accuracy and short-term stock performance. However, there is consensus that the space industry is experiencing significant growth.

title: Symphony Space Unveils Adagio-XL ODC Sat
summary: Symphony Space has introduced the Adagio-XL ODC Sat, entering the orbital data center market. The company aims to leverage space-based infrastructure for data processing and storage.

title: Orbital Raises $5M Pre-Seed to Build ODC Constellation
summary: Orbital secures $5 million in pre-seed funding to develop its ODC constellation, which uses smaller satellites to handle large AI inference workloads and maintain constant ground communication.

title: FCC Gives Amazon Leo More Time to Launch
summary: The FCC has extended the deadline for Amazon's Project Kuiper to deploy at least half of its satellite constellation, while imposing conditions to expedite launches. The decision aims to balance regulatory flexibility with encouraging timely deployment.

title: Artemis III Astronauts Will Meet SpaceX, Blue Origin Landers in Earth Orbit
summary: NASA confirmed that SpaceX and Blue Origin will each provide a lander for the Artemis III mission's Earth orbit test in 2027, serving as a precursor for future Moon landings. The event marked a key step in NASA's Artemis program.

title: Reflex Aerospace Taps Arkadia Space for 2027 Mission
summary: Reflex Aerospace has chosen Arkadia Space, a Spanish startup, to supply a propulsion system for its satellite mission scheduled for 2027.

title: SpaceX Begins Trading at $150 Making Elon Musk the World’s First Trillionaire on Paper
summary: SpaceX started public trading on Nasdaq at $150 per share, an 11% increase over its initial IPO price, making Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire on paper.

title: Satellite IoT is Becoming Essential Infrastructure as NTN and Cellular IoT Services Converge
summary: Satellite IoT is increasingly recognized as essential infrastructure as Non-Terrestrial Networks (NTN) and cellular IoT services converge. This integration is critical for enterprises managing fleets, utilities, and remote assets.

title: U.S. State Department Taps SpaceX’s Starlink for Two-Year Disaster Response Initiative
summary: The U.S. Department of State signed a two-year agreement with SpaceX to use Starlink for disaster response and humanitarian aid. This initiative aims to enhance international disaster preparedness.

title: Canadian Government Awards EO Satellite Contracts to Calian, Kepler, MDA Space
summary: The Canadian government awarded three Earth Observation (EO) satellite contracts worth C$2.4 million to Calian, Kepler, and MDA Space to enhance its EO capabilities.

title: Viasat Secures PTS-G U.S. Space Force SSC Swarm 1 Contract
summary: Viasat has secured a contract from the U.S. Space Force’s Space Systems Command to build, launch, and deliver the first satellite in a proliferated fleet for the Swarm 1 program. This contract marks a significant step in Viasat's involvement in U.S. military space initiatives.

title: SpaceX Set for IPO, Banking on Faith in Elon Musk and Massive AI Growth
summary: SpaceX is preparing for its IPO on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol SPCX, leveraging investor confidence in Elon Musk and the anticipated growth of AI. The company, which has operated privately for 24 years, aims to capitalize on its reputation and future potential.

title: Applied Atomics Emerges from Stealth with Enough Backing to Build Star Reacher Network
summary: Applied Atomics has come out of stealth mode with over $500 million in demand commitments to develop the Star Reacher network, a mobility infrastructure layer. The company aims to build this network to support future space missions and infrastructure.

title: Space Forge Secures £10 Million of New Investment through European, UK Space Agencies
summary: Space Forge secured £10 million ($13.4 million) in new investment from the European Space Agency and UK Space Agency. The funding comes through ESA's General Support Technology Programme and UKSA's investment.

title: Lithuania and Luxembourg Strengthen Space Defense Ties
summary: Lithuania and Luxembourg are strengthening their collaboration in space defense following a meeting between their respective defense ministers. The agreement aims to enhance cooperation in the strategic space sector.

title: Another Earth Expands Into Brazil
summary: Another Earth, an Earth Observation data company, is expanding its operations into Brazil, a key market in Latin America. The move is driven by Brazil's climate challenges and economic growth.
